new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling fermented beverages (e.g. cider, perry, mead, saké) and mixtures of fermented beverages and non-alcoholic beverages, not elsewhere specified or included, that exhibit the sparkling (effervescent) character as defined by additional note 10 to Chapter 22 (excess pressure ≥ 3 bar at 20 °C, determined by mushroom stopper or manometer), presented in containers of 2 litres or less.

coverage_explanation: The three primary discriminating criteria for CN 2206 00 31 are captured: (1) the beverage belongs to CN 2206 (other fermented beverages — not wine of fresh grapes, not beer, not vermouth, etc.), encoded via the parent class OtherFermentedBeverages2206 and the FermentationProcess2206 restriction; (2) it is "sparkling" as defined by additional note 10 to Chapter 22 (excess pressure ≥ 3 bar at 20 °C, identified by mushroom stopper or manometer test), encoded via SparklingWineQuality; (3) it is presented in containers of 2 litres or less, encoded via SmallContainer. The legal note cross-references additional note 10 to Chapter 22 and the explanatory notes to subheadings 2204 21 06–2204 21 09 for the mushroom-stopper definition; these definitional criteria are only partially representable in the current TBox because SparklingWineQuality is a named quality class whose full definition (≥ 3 bar at 20 °C) is not axiomatised with a numeric pressure range in the present TBox (no pressure datatype restriction is available as a computable axiom in this session). The sibling 22060039 (sparkling, containers > 2 L) is discriminated by the SmallContainer vs. LargeContainer restriction. Coverage is reduced slightly because the mushroom-stopper sub-criterion and the exact pressure threshold are referenced by cross-note only and cannot be fully encoded without additional TBox infrastructure.
